title: Catalyst Watch: Big bank earnings, major healthcare + retail conferences, Fedspeak flurry, CPI
content:
[Panoramic skyline of New York city at sunset, USA]
Nico De Pasquale Photography

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - JANUARY 12

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - AST SpaceMobile (ASTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ASTS]) and Oklo (OKLO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OKLO]) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Amicus (FOLD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FOLD]), Shattuck Labs (STTK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STTK]), and Dynavax (DVAX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DVAX]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include CVR Energy (CVI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CVI]), Lamb Weston (LW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LW]), and AudioEye (AEYE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AEYE]). 
 	* IPO WATCH - No significant IPOs are scheduled for the week. The analyst quiet period expires on Medline (MDLN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MDLN]), Andersen Group (ANDG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ANDG]), and Starfighters Space (FJET [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FJET]) to free up analysts to post ratings. The IPO lockup period expires for blocks of shares of Delixy (DLXY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DLXY]) and Masonglory (MSGY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSGY]).
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include AT&T (T [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/T]), Verizon (VZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VZ]), Comcast (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]), and Abbott Labs (ABT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABT]).
 	* SPOTLIGHT ON ICR - One of the biggest retail conferences of the year will take place. The ICR Conference typically includes a wave of holiday sales updates and full-year guidance announcements. Notable companies participating include Walmart (WMT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WMT]), Urban Outfitters (URBN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/URBN]), Shake Shack (SHAK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SHAK]), Movado (MOV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MOV]), and Yeti Holdings (YETI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/YETI]).
 	* ALL DAY - The four-day JPMorgan Healthcare Conference begins in San Francisco. The conference is considered one of the most important of the year in the healthcare sector. Johnson & Johnson (JNJ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JNJ]), Novartis (NVS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVS]), AstraZeneca (AZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AZN]), Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BMY]), and Kodiak Sciences (KOD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KOD]) are some of the notable companies making presentations. FDA Commissioner Marty Makary will give a dedicated keynote talk on regulatory priorities, accelerated approvals, AI in drug development, and safety/efficacy standards.
 	* ALL DAY - The National Retail Federation's Big Show event will take place in New York City. BJ's Wholesale Club (BJ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BJ]) CEO Robert Eddy, Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DKS]) Executive Chairman Ed Stack, and Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ANF]) CEO Fran Horowitz will be giving speeches or participating in fireside chats.
 	* ALL DAY - The USDA will issue its monthly World Agricultural Supply & Demand Estimates report. WASDE includes U.S. and world supply-demand tables for wheat, rice, coarse grains (such as corn, sorghum, barley, oats), oilseeds (notably soybeans), and cotton, plus some coverage of sugar and livestock.
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders of Avadel Pharmaceuticals (AVDL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AVDL]) vote on the buyout offer from Alkermes (ALKS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ALKS]). AVDL traded about 4% below the buyout price of $22.50 at the time of publication.
 	* 10:35 A.M. St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem will speak on the U.S. economy and monetary policy before an MNI webinar.
 	* 6:00 P.M. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will give a keynote before the C. Peter McColough Series on International Economics event organized by the Council on Foreign Relations.

TUESDAY - JANUARY 13

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include JPMorgan Chase (JPM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JPM]), Bank of New York Mellon (BK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BK]), and Delta Air Lines (DAL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DAL]).
 	* ALL DAY - The three-day Needham Growth Conference features a long list of companies presenting, including PagerDuty (PD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PD]), Surf Air Mobility (SRFM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SRFM]), Bitfarms (BITF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BITF]), and Navitas (NVTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVTS]).
 	* ALL DAY - It is the five-year anniversary of Affirm Holdings' (AFRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AFRM]) IPO. The stock traded 67% above the IPO price of $49 at the time of publication.
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with Denny's (DENN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DENN]) will vote on the deal to be taken private by TriArtisan Capital Advisors (owner of TGI Fridays and P.F. Chang's).
 	* 8:30 A.M. The Consumer Price Index report for December will be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. Economists expect the core inflation rate to remain level at 2.7%.
 	* 9:00 A.M. The New Home Sales report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.

WEDNESDAY - JANUARY 14

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Bank of America (BAC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BAC]), Wells Fargo (WFC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WFC]), Citigroup (C [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/C]), and H.B. Fuller (FUL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FUL]).
 	* ALL DAY - The Detroit Auto Show will include press conferences and model reveals from major automakers. The winners of the 2026 North American Car, Truck and Utility Vehicle of the Year Awards will also be announced.
 	* ALL DAY - OPEC will publish its monthly oil market report.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The U.S. Census Bureau is scheduled to release the November Retail Sales report.
 	* 9:50 A.M. Philadelphia Federal Reserve Bank President Anna Paulson speaks on the economic outlook before the Chamber of Commerce for Greater Philadelphia State of the Economy event.
 	* 2:00 P.M. The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.
 	* 2:10 P.M. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will give opening remarks before an event organized by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

THURSDAY - JANUARY 15

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Morgan Stanley (MS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MS]), Goldman Sachs (GS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GS]), BlackRock (BLK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BLK]), and JB Hunt Transport (JBHT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JBHT]).
 	* ALL DAY - Costco (COST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COST]) will hold its annual meeting. Shareholders will vote on the usual director elections, auditor ratification, say-on-pay, and at least one shareholder proposal related to ESG/climate issues. There has been speculation that Costco (COST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COST]) may announce a special dividend this year.
 	* ALL DAY - Traders have their eyes on the release of the Cocoa Association of Asia's quarterly grindings report.
 	* ALL DAY - Bank credit card metric reports will be closely watched for signs that consumers are buckling to financial pressure. American Express (AXP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AXP]), Bank of America (BAC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BAC]), Citigroup (C [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/C]), Capital One Financial (COF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COF]), Discover Financial (DFS), JPMorgan (JPM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JPM]), and Synchrony Financial (SYF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SYF]) are all due to update on credit card net charge-offs and delinquency rates for December.
 	* ALL DAY - Topgolf Callaway (MODG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MODG]) will officially revert to its old name, Callaway Golf, following the sale of a controlling stake of the Topgolf business.

FRIDAY - JANUARY 16

	* ALL DAY - It is the last day before the FOMC blackout period ahead of the next meeting begins. Fed Vice Chair Philip Jefferson, Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman, Fed Governor Michael Barr, and Fed Governor Stephen Miran all have talks planned.

_Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story._

MORE ON THE MARKETS

 	* 2026 Forecast: Don't Fight The Fed Until It's Dead [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4858379-2026-forecast-dont-fight-the-fed-until-its-dead]
 	* Is The Infamous 'Wall Of Worry' Finally Crumbling? [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4858370-is-the-infamous-wall-of-worry-finally-crumbling]
 	* AllianceBernstein sees steady growth in 2026, but flags rising volatility and policy risks [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4538106-alliancebernstein-sees-steady-growth-in-2026-but-flags-rising-volatility-and-policy-risks]
 	* January Fed rate cut bets dip after latest labor report [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4538042-january-fed-rate-cut-bets-dip-after-latest-labor-report]

title: Delixy Holdings HY Profit Increases, But Revenue Decreases
content:
(RTTNews) - Delixy Holdings Limited (DLXY), a Singapore-based company engaged in the trading of oil related products, reported that its net income for the six months ended June 30, 2025 increased to $560 thousand or $0.037 per share from $450 thousand or $0.030 per share in the same period last year. But revenues for the period were $102.0 million, a decrease from $143.8 million for the same period last year. The decrease was primarily attributable to the lower selling price, following weak oil demand and lower international oil price in first half of 2025. For more earnings news, earnings calendar, and earnings for stocks, visit rttnews.com.

The views and opinions expressed herein are the views and opinions of the author and do not necessarily reflect those of Nasdaq, Inc.

title: Catalyst Watch: Palantir earnings, Firefly Aerospace IPO, OPEC+, and the Fed's SLOOS report
content:
[Stock Chart Between Skyscrapers - Financial Analysis, Trading, Investment]
DKosig

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

SUNDAY - AUGUST 3

	* OIL WATCH - A OPEC+ meeting will bring together eight key oil-producing countries (Saudi Arabia, Russia, Iraq, UAE, Kuwait, Kazakhstan, Algeria, and Oman) to review and potentially set oil production targets for September. The meeting follows a strategic shift where those members have rapidly increased oil output over spring and summer 2025, seeking to regain market share amid strong seasonal demand. 

MONDAY - AUGUST 4

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - Opendoor Technologies (OPEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OPEN]) and New Fortress Energy (NFE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFE]) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Celcuity (CELC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CELC]), Eqv Ventures Acquisition Corp. II (EVAC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EVAC]), and Rich Sparkle (ANPA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ANPA]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include NextNRG (NXXT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NXXT]), Boss Energy (OTCQX:BQSSF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BQSSF]), and Charter Communications (CHTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CHTR]).
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Palantir Technologies (PLTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PLTR]), MercadoLibre (MELI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MELI]), Wayfair (W [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/W]), Tyson Foods (TSN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TSN]), and Williams Companies (WMB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WMB]).
 	* IPO WATCH - Firefly Aerospace (FLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FLY]) is expected to price its IPO and start trading during the week. The Texas-based space and defense technology company is looking to raise up to $631.8 million by selling 16.2 million shares of common stock at an expected price range of $35 to $39 per share. The analyst quiet periods expire on Ten-League International (TLIH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TLIH]), Delixy Holdings (DLXY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DLXY]), and Almonty Industries (ALM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ALM]) to free up analysts to post ratings.
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Citigroup (C [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/C]), Las Vegas Sands (LVS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LVS]), MetLife (MET [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MET]), and IBM (IBM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IBM]).
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders of Vigil Neuroscience (VIGL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VIGL]) will vote on the deal for the company to be acquired by Sanofi (SNY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNY]).
 	* 10:00 A.M. The FDA will hold a significant public meeting about the reauthorization of the Medical Device User Fee Amendments.
 	* 10:00 A.M. The June Durable Goods Orders will be released. Economists forecast a 9% year-over-year decline.
 	* 2:00 P.M. The Federal Reserve's Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey on Bank Lending Practices will be released. The SLOOS report will provide an update on the conditions, supply and demand of bank loans extended to customers in the U.S. Tightening standards can signal stress in the banking system or broader economy, impacting financial stocks.
 	* 5:00 P.M. Palantir Technologies (PLTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PLTR]) will hold its earnings conference call. Analysts think the call could include color on the new U.S. Army deal. Options trading implies a 12% move in the stock after the earnings report and call.

TUESDAY - AUGUST 5

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Advanced Micro Devices (AMD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMD]), Caterpillar (CAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CAT]), Amgen (AMGN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMGN]), Arista Networks (ANET [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ANET]), Pfizer (PFE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PFE]), Diageo (DEO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DEO]), Yum! Brands (YUM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/YUM]), and BP (BP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BP]). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Opendoor Technologies (OPEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OPEN]), Upstart (UPST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/UPST]), and Lemonade (LMND [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LMND]) after their reports are released.
 	* ALL DAY - Alibaba (BABA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BABA]) will hold its annual meeting. Shareholders will vote on proposals to grant the board mandates to issue new shares and repurchase shares. The tech giant could also update on the outlook for the artificial intelligence and cloud computing divisions.
 	* 11:00 A.M. The Treasury Department will make a buyback announcement. The preliminary announcement typically includes the maximum amount of securities the Treasury is planning to repurchase, the range of maturities, a list of eligible securities, as well as the date and time of the operation and settlement.
 	* 5:00 P.M. AMD (AMD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMD]) will hold its earnings conference call. The update could impact GlobalFoundries (GFS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GFS]), which generates 11% of its revenue from AMD and trades in tandem with it 88% of the time in the week after earnings.

WEDNESDAY - AUGUST 6

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include McDonald's (MCD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCD]), Novo Nordisk (NVO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVO]), Disney (DIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DIS]), Uber Technologies (UBER [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/UBER]), Shopify (SHOP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SHOP]), DoorDash (DASH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DASH]), McKesson (MCK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCK]), Airbnb (ABNB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABNB]), Fortinet (FTNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FTNT]), and MetLife (MET [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MET]). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Solid Power (SLDP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SLDP]), Beyond Meat (BYND [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BYND]), and Sunrun (RUN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RUN]) after their earnings are released.
 	* ALL DAY - Eledon Pharmaceuticals (ELDN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ELDN]) will present at the World Transplant Congress updated clinical data from its ongoing open-label Phase 1b study evaluating tegoprubart for the prevention of rejection in subjects undergoing kidney transplantation.
 	* ALL DAY - Federal Reserve Board Governor Lisa Cook and Boston Fed President Susan Collins will participate in a panel discussion titled "A central bank perspective on the evolving global landscape."
 	* 8:30 A.M. McDonald's (MCD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCD]) will hold its earnings call. Analysts think the company's update on consumer trends could have implications across the restaurant sector. Lamb Weston (LW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LW]) will also be watched closely since the company derives 15% of its revenue from McDonald's and trades in tandem with its 75% of the time in the week after earnings.
 	* 3:10 P.M. San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly will speak and participate in a moderated conversation before the 2025 Anchorage Economic Summit.

THURSDAY - AUGUST 7

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Eli Lilly (LLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LLY]), Gilead Sciences (GILD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GILD]), ConocoPhillips (COP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COP]), Block (XYZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XYZ]), and Monster Beverage (MNST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MNST]). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Realreal (REAL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/REAL]) and Sweetgreen (SG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SG]) after their earnings are released.
 	* ALL DAY - Tariff developments will be front and center for market watchers as a new set of U.S. tariffs becomes payable.
 	* ALL DAY - The European Central Bank will publish its economic bulletin.
 	* 7:00 A.M. - The Bank of England will announce the decision from its Monetary Policy Committee on the UK interest rate and broader monetary policy.
 	* 10:00 A.M. - Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic will participate in a moderated conversation with Ira Birns, president and chief financial officer, World Kinect Corporation (WKC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WKC]).

FRIDAY - AUGUST 8

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include EOG Resources (EOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EOG]), Wendy's (WEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WEN]), and Tempus AI (TEM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TEM]).
 	* ALL DAY - The United Nations will release its FAO food price index. The report includes 24 major agricultural commodities grouped into five main indices covering meat, dairy, cereals, vegetable oils, and sugar.
 	* ALL DAY - Take-Two Interactive (TTWO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TTWO]) is set to launch _Mafia: The Country_ globally. The release is one of the most anticipated videogame titles of the back half of the year.
 	* 10:20 A.M. St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem will speak before an event hosted by Mississippi Valley State University.

MORE ON THE MARKETS 

 	* The AI Boom Vs. The Economic 'Kryptonite' Of High-Interest Rates And Tariffs [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4807592-the-ai-boom-vs-the-economic-kryptonite-of-high-interest-rates-and-tariffs]
 	* July Jobs Report Stuns Wall Street, Fed Rate Cut In Play, Key Charts [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4807565-july-jobs-report-stuns-wall-street-fed-rate-cut-in-play-key-charts]
 	* A Sniff Of Stagflation Stalls The Rally [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4807545-a-sniff-of-stagflation-stalls-the-rally]
 	* SA Sentiment: Will the Fed only cut interest rates once in 2025? [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4476369-sa-sentiment-fed-rate-cut-expectations]
 	* Barclays continues to prefer U.S. equities over rest of the world [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4476501-barclays-continues-to-prefer-us-equities-over-rest-of-the-world]

title: Delixy Holdings Limited Announces Closing of Initial Public Offering
content:
Singapore, July  10, 2025  (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Delixy Holdings Limited (Nasdaq: DLXY) (the “Company” or “Delixy”), a Singapore-based company engaged in the trading of oil related products, today announced the closing of its initial public offering (the “Offering”) of 2,000,000 ordinary shares, par value US$0.000005 per share, (“Ordinary Shares”), 1,350,000 of which were offered by the Company and 650,000 by the selling shareholders Mega Origin Holdings Limited (as to 325,000 Ordinary Shares) and Novel Majestic Limited (as to 325,000 Ordinary Shares) (the “Selling Shareholders”), at a public offering price of US$4.00 per Ordinary Share, raising total gross proceeds of US$8 million in the aggregate to the Company and Selling Shareholders.

The Ordinary Shares began trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market on July 9, 2025 under the ticker symbol “DLXY.”

The Company also registered a resale prospectus concurrent with the Offering for the resale of 3,000,000 Ordinary Shares held by Cosmic Magnet Limited, Rosywood Holdings Limited, Dragon Circle Limited, Novel Majestic Limited, and Golden Legend Ventures Limited (the “Resale Shareholders”).

The Company received aggregate gross proceeds of US$5.4 million from the Offering, before deducting underwriting discounts and other related expenses. The Company did not receive any proceeds from the sale of Ordinary Shares offered by the Selling Shareholders or Resale Shareholders in the Offering.

Proceeds from the Offering will be used for: (i) expanding product offerings; (ii) strengthening market position; (iii) potentially making strategic acquisitions and business cooperations, including joint ventures and/or strategic alliances and (iv) general working capital and corporate purposes.

The Offering was conducted on a firm commitment basis. Bancroft Capital, LLC acted as the sole lead underwriter for the Offering. Ortoli Rosenstadt LLP acted as U.S. counsel to the Company, led by William S. Rosenstadt and Mengyi “Jason” Ye, and Nelson Mullins Riley & Scarborough LLP acted as U.S. counsel to the Underwriters, led by W. David Mannheim, Ashley Wu and Kathryn Simons, in connection with the Offering.

A registration statement on Form F-1 relating to the Offering was filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the “SEC”) (File Number: 333-283248), as amended, and was declared effective by the SEC on July 8, 2025. The Offering was made only by means of a prospectus, forming a part of the registration statement. Copies of the final prospectus relating to the Offering may be obtained from Bancroft Capital, LLC, 501 Office Center Drive, Suite 130, Fort Washington, PA 19034, or by telephone at +1 (484) 546-8000. In addition, copies of the final prospectus relating to the Offering may be obtained via the SEC's website at www.sec.gov.

This press release does not constitute an offer to sell, or the solicitation of an offer to buy any of the Company’s securities, nor shall such securities be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an applicable exemption from registration, nor shall there be any offer, solicitation or sale of any of the Company’s securities in any state or jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of such state or jurisdiction.

About Delixy Holdings Limited

Delixy Holdings Limited is a Singapore-based company principally engaged in the trading of oil-related products, including (i) crude oil and (ii) oil-based products such as fuel oils, motor gasoline, additives, gas condensate, base oils, asphalt, petrochemicals and naphtha (heavy gasoline). Operating across multiple countries in Southeast Asia, East Asia, and Middle East, Delixy has established a strong presence in the region’s oil trading markets. While Delixy maintains a diversified portfolio of oil products, crude oil trading represents a core aspect of its business. The Company leverages its strong existing relationships with customers and suppliers as well as deep industry expertise to provide value-added services, including tailored recommendations on optimal trading strategies and shipping and logistical support where required. In addition, the Company’s financing capabilities allow it to extend credit terms to customers while satisfying suppliers’ immediate payment terms. For more information, please visit the Company’s website: https://ir.delixy.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

Certain statements in this announcement are forward-looking statements. These forward-looking statements involve known and unknown risks and uncertainties and are based on the Company’s current expectations and projections about future events that the Company believes may affect its financial condition, results of operations, business strategy, and financial needs. Investors can find many (but not all) of these statements by the use of words such as “believe”, “plan”, “expect”, “intend”, “should”, “seek”, “estimate”, “will”, “aim” and “anticipate” or other similar expressions in this prospectus. The Company undertakes no obligation to update or revise publicly any forward-looking statements to reflect subsequent occurring events or circumstances, or changes in its expectations, except as may be required by law. Although the Company believes that the expectations expressed in these forward-looking statements are reasonable, it cannot assure you that such expectations will turn out to be correct, and the Company cautions investors that actual results may differ materially from the anticipated results and encourages investors to review other factors that may affect its future results in the Registration Statement and other filings with the SEC.

For media inquiries, please contact:

Delixy Holdings Limited
Investor Relations Department
Email: ir@delixy.com

Ascent Investor Relations LLC
Tina Xiao
Phone: +1-646-932-7242
Email: investors@ascent-ir.com
